run.pathName: Parameter Inference v2 No Param Add
run.errorMessage: Database write affected 0 records for update (updateOne).

rules.title: Path has trigger node
rules.description: A runnable path must include at least one Trigger node.
rules.recommendation: Add a Trigger node and connect it to the workflow entry branch.

rules.title: Simulation requires entity ID and mapped collection
rules.description: Simulation nodes used for preflight/testing need an entity ID and a resolvable collection map entry.
rules.recommendation: Set Simulation.entityId (or productId) and define mapping for Simulation.entityType in Validation settings.

rules.title: Database query has collection
rules.description: Database nodes must target a valid collection.
rules.recommendation: Choose a known collection in Database node config or provide a valid custom collection.

rules.title: Database update/delete has identity input
rules.description: Write operations should have explicit identity wiring to avoid hidden fallback updates.
rules.recommendation: Wire Parser/Context output into Database.entityId (or productId) for deterministic writes.

name.en: Silksong | 13 cm | Faux Leather | Gaming Wallet | Hollow Knight
name.pl: Silksong | 13 cm | Faux Leather | Portfel Gamingowy | Hollow Knight

description.en: The pale light of Pharloom guides the way, even beyond Hallownest. This 13cm wallet, crafted from durable faux leather, showcases a striking scene of familiar figures—the Knight, Hornet, and a host of curious bugs—embroiled in the mysteries of *Silksong*. Vivid reds and deep blacks swirl across the design, highlighting the iconic “SILKSONG” lettering alongside the silhouette of a blade. With multiple interior slots for essential geo and cards, it’s a practical way to carry a piece of the kingdom with you. Perfect for everyday use or displaying your devotion to the endless cycle.

Rules:
1. Return ONLY valid JSON array.
2. Output schema per item: {"parameterId":"<id>","value":"<english value>"}.
3. parameterId MUST exactly match an "id" from Available parameters JSON.
4. Never invent semantic IDs like "size", "material", "color".
5. Skip parameters you cannot infer confidently.
6. No markdown, no explanations, no code fences.
7. If nothing can be inferred, return [].
8. For any multi-value parameter, return ONE string joined with "|" and no spaces around "|", e.g. "Black|Red|Blue".
9. Capitalize value tokens in Title Case (e.g. "Black", "Stainless Steel").

name.pl: Silksong | 13 cm | Faux Leather | Portfel Gamingowy | Hollow Knight

description.en: The pale light of Pharloom guides the way, even beyond Hallownest. This 13cm wallet, crafted from durable faux leather, showcases a striking scene of familiar figures—the Knight, Hornet, and a host of curious bugs—embroiled in the mysteries of *Silksong*. Vivid reds and deep blacks swirl across the design, highlighting the iconic “SILKSONG” lettering alongside the silhouette of a blade. With multiple interior slots for essential geo and cards, it’s a practical way to carry a piece of the kingdom with you. Perfect for everyday use or displaying your devotion to the endless cycle.

nodes.title: JSON Parser
nodes.description: Extract fields into outputs or a single bundle.

notes.text: Grab product name, description and images.

nodes.title: Database Query
nodes.description: Load available parameters for product catalog (with fallback by product parameter IDs).

notes.text: Grabs the catalog ID For given product

prompt.template:
Infer product PARAMETERS in ENGLISH from the product data below.
Product name: "{{title}}"
Product description: "{{content_en}}"
Available parameters JSON (objects with id/label/selectorType/options): {{result}}

Rules:
1. Return ONLY valid JSON array.
2. Output schema per item: {"parameterId":"<id>","value":"<english value>"}.
3. parameterId MUST exactly match an "id" from Available parameters JSON.
4. Never invent semantic IDs like "size", "material", "color".
5. Skip parameters you cannot infer confidently.
6. No markdown, no explanations, no code fences.
7. If nothing can be inferred, return [].
8. For any multi-value parameter, return ONE string joined with "|" and no spaces around "|", e.g. "Black|Red|Blue".
9. Capitalize value tokens in Title Case (e.g. "Black", "Stainless Steel").

nodes.title: Model
nodes.description: Run vision model to infer parameters.

nodes.title: Regex JSON Extract
nodes.description: Extract JSON array from model response.

nodes.title: Database Query
nodes.description: Update product parameters with inferred values.

nodes.title: Fetcher: Trigger Context
nodes.description: Resolve live trigger context or fetch simulated entity by ID.

rules.title: Path has trigger node
rules.description: A runnable path must include at least one Trigger node.
rules.recommendation: Add a Trigger node and connect it to the workflow entry branch.

rules.title: Simulation requires entity ID and mapped collection
rules.description: Simulation nodes used for preflight/testing need an entity ID and a resolvable collection map entry.
rules.recommendation: Set Simulation.entityId (or productId) and define mapping for Simulation.entityType in Validation settings.

rules.title: Database query has collection
rules.description: Database nodes must target a valid collection.
rules.recommendation: Choose a known collection in Database node config or provide a valid custom collection.

rules.title: Database update/delete has identity input
rules.description: Write operations should have explicit identity wiring to avoid hidden fallback updates.
rules.recommendation: Wire Parser/Context output into Database.entityId (or productId) for deterministic writes.
